title: glechoma shikikunensis
intro: Glechoma shikikunensis, also known as Japanese mint, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Glechoma shikikunensis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant has been used to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory problems, offering various health benefits to those who consume it. Glechoma shikikunensis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used fresh in cooking and herbal remedies.
